state.name: Delaware
state.agency: Delaware Division of Fish and Wildlife
state.peak_intrusion_season: March through May for denning, and again September through November as animals seek winter shelter
state.climate_factor: Delaware's mild but humid climate keeps wildlife active year-round, with shorter dormancy periods than in northern states
state.permit_note: Most nuisance wildlife in Delaware can be trapped by licensed contractors year-round, but relocation of certain species requires state authorization

New Castle County.region: northern Delaware along the Delaware River
New Castle County.habitat_type: mixed urban, suburban, and Piedmont woodland
New Castle County.regional_wildlife: groundhogs are abundant in suburban edge habitats
New Castle County.absent_or_rare: armadillos and coyotes remain uncommon compared to states further south and west

Kent County.region: central Delaware between the Chesapeake and Delaware Bays
Kent County.habitat_type: agricultural lowlands, coastal marsh, and small woodlots
Kent County.regional_wildlife: snapping turtles and water snakes around the many farm ponds and tidal waterways
Kent County.absent_or_rare: tree squirrels are less concentrated here than in the wooded northern county

Sussex County.region: southern Delaware along the Atlantic coast and Delaware Bay
Sussex County.habitat_type: coastal pine forest, salt marsh, and Atlantic beach communities
Sussex County.regional_wildlife: bats roost heavily in coastal vacation homes and outbuildings, and ospreys often nest on residential structures
Sussex County.absent_or_rare: moose and porcupines do not occur this far south
